LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAPAZOTE COLE, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATIONIS GRANTED. IN THE EVENT THAT MR. HERNANDEZ DOES APPEAR BEFORE THE COURT THIS IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ANY FURTHER ARGUMENTS THAT MS. MEDINA MAY WISH TO MAKE ONCE HE IS BEFORE THE COURT. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: S. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAPAZOTE COLE, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 13, 2019 TO GIVE THE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO TAKE DISCOVERY LIMITED TO THE ISSUE OF THE AUTHENTICITY OF THE PURPORTED SIGNATURE OF PLAINTIFF CARLA MEDINA ON THE MUTUAL AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE CLAIMS. ALL SUCH DISCOVERY MUST BE COMPLETED BY MAY 14, 2019. MS. MEDINA MAY FILE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION PAPERS NO LATER THAN MAY 24, 2019. PAPAZOTE MAY FILE SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY PAPERS NO LATER THAN JUNE 5, 2019. WHILE MS. MEDINA DOES NOT HAVE A RIGHT TO TAKE DISCOVERY, THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO ALLOW HER (AND PAPAZOTE) TO DO SO. MS. MEDINA HAS SHOWN THAT SHE HAS SUFFICIENT DOUBT ABOUT THE AUTHENTICITY OF HER PURPORTED SIGNATURE THAT FAIRNESS SUPPORTS ALLOWING DISCOVERY ON THAT ISSUE. PAPAZOTE'S ARGUMENT THAT MS. MEDINA IS OBLIGATED TO ARBITRATE HER CLAIMS EVEN IF SHE DIDN'T SIGN THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT LACKS MERIT. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Feb-14-2019 Continued To Jun-13-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mathew Stephenson, Esq., Kochan & Stephenson, Telephone 501-649-1130, appearing for Plaintiff;
Brooke Purcell, Esq., Sheppard Mullin, Telephone 415-434-9100, appearing for Defendant Papazotte Cole,

Ruling - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PAPAZOTE COLE, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Defendant Papazote Cole, LLC's motion to compel arbitration is continued to June 13, 2019 to give the parties an opportunity to take discovery limited to the issue of the authenticity of the purported signature of plaintiff Carla Medina on the Mutual Agreement to Arbitrate Claims. All such discovery must be completed by May 14, 2019. Ms. Medina may file supplemental opposition papers no later than May 24, 2019. Papazote may file supplemental reply papers no later than June 5, 2019. While Ms. Medina does not have a right to take discovery, the court exercises its discretion to allow her (and Papazote) to do so. Ms. Medina has shown that she has sufficient doubt about the authenticity of her purported signature that fairness supports allowing discovery on that issue. Papazote's argument that Ms. Medina is obligated to arbitrate her claims even if she didn't sign the Arbitration Agreement lacks merit.

Plaintiff's Counsel is required to prepare a form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CARLA MEDINA's Motion To Compel Deposition And Production Of Documents

The motion to compel is taken off calendar until such time as the Court rules on Papazote Colle, LLC's motion to compel arbitration. Should the motion be denied, moving party Carla Medina may renotice the motion on a future date convenient to both parties.
